description: Vertical full-color candid photograph taken outdoors in a parking lot (autumn-colored trees, dark vehicles, other figures in the blurred background). A man seen from behind wears a black short-sleeve shirt with a graffiti or flame print and blue jeans with bright green-and-black sneakers, his hands cuffed behind his back. He is flanked by two officers: at left a plainclothes officer (dark cap, dark t-shirt, tan trousers) and at right a uniformed law-enforcement officer in a tan/khaki shirt and trousers (a Florida Highway Patrol trooper, with a visible shoulder patch, duty belt and holstered sidearm) holding the man's arm. There is no on-image graphic text, headline, slogan, seal or logo overlay. Context from the tweet: USCIS states it took into custody a person described as an illegal alien from Cuba (who it says entered fraudulently in 2004, had a 2008 conviction, and was ordered removed in 2012) when he came to the Orlando office, and credits ICE and Florida Highway Patrol.
